Document:
# Avali
[bold]Avali[/bold] are a [bold][color=#f44]Nitrogen-breathing[/color][/bold], [bold][color=#94f]Ammonia-blooded[/color][/bold] species; with two big eyes and four long ears, covered head to toe in fine feathers.
Avali have a faster metabolism than most species, their bodies [bold]process reagents 30% faster[/bold] than baseline and [bold]get hungry twice as fast[/bold]. Their specialized and overclocked organs are more taxed by certain compounds, suffering [bold][color=#4df]Airloss[/color] damage[/bold] from Ethanol.
Avali have a [bold]lightweight build[/bold], with a lower body mass; the most immediate consequence being [bold]difficulty dragging objects[/bold] and [bold]being easier dragged by others[/bold].
Avali physiology is [bold]highly adapted to [color=#4df]low temperatures[/color][/bold], having a baseline body temperature of [bold][color=yellow]22°C[/color][/bold] (~[color=yellow]296°K[/color]). They experience the effects of [bold]hypothermia at [color=#4df]-50°C[/color][/bold] and [bold]hyperthermia at [color=#f44]37°C[/color][/bold]. (~[color=#4df]223°K[/color] & [color=#f44]310°K[/color])
Avali are [bold]omnivorous[/bold], meaning they can safely digest [bold]meat and plant based foods[/bold], but they [bold]can't stomach raw meat or blood[/bold].
Their bodies can process [bold]2 reagents[/bold] at a time.
They slowly [bold]passively regenerate[/bold] all types of [bold][color=yellow]Brute[/color] damage[/bold], and individually [bold][color=#f44]Heat[/color] damage[/bold].
Avali names are typically [bold]two to three syllables[/bold], frequenting nasal [color=#aaa](/m, n/)[/color] and stop [color=#aaa](/k, t/)[/color] sounds.
